In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 35A read with Section 56 of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949, as amended vide Banking Regulation (Amendment) Act 2020 (39
of 2020), and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of India ('RBI') in this
regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to
do, hereby, issues the Directions hereinafter specified.

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text:
A. Short Title and Commencement
1.
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025.
2.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
Provided that for Urban Co-operative Banks covered under the scope of these
Directions as enumerated in paragraph 3, their existing Information Technology(IT)
outsourcing agreements regardless of whether they are due for renewal on or after
the effective date of these Directions shall comply with the provisions of these
Directions either at the time of renewal or by April 10, 2026, whichever is earlier.
However, the bank's new IT outsourcing agreements that come into force on or after
the effective date of these Directions, shall comply with the provisions of these
Directions from the date of agreement itself.
Provided further that nothing in the preceding proviso shall be construed as
permitting non-compliance with any other extant regulatory instructions or statutory
requirements applicable to such arrangements.

children.title: B. Applicability and Scope
children.text:
B. Applicability and Scope
3.
These Directions shall be applicable to all Urban Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank).
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Urban Co-operative Banks' means Primary
Co-operative Banks as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of
Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Provided that Chapter IV and IT-specific provisions contained in Chapter II of these
Directions shall apply only to Tier-3 and Tier-4 Urban Co-operative Banks as
defined in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Licensing,
Scheduling and Regulatory Classification) Guidelines, 2025.

children.title: Explanation: While Chapter IV as a whole is applicable exclusively to Tier-3 and
children.text:
Explanation: While Chapter IV as a whole is applicable exclusively to Tier-3 and
Tier-4 Urban Co-operative Banks, certain IT-related provisions in Chapter II (e.g.,
Board-level IT outsourcing policy and responsibilities of and reviews by the Board
with respect to IT outsourcing) are also intended for these categories only. The
remaining provisions of Chapter II are applicable to all Urban Co-operative Banks,
including those in Tier-1 and Tier-2.

children.title: 4. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter III shall be as under:
children.text:
4. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter III shall be as under:
(1) The provisions shall apply to outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank with
a service provider, located in India or elsewhere, for outsourcing of financial
services like applications processing (loan origination, credit card), document
processing, marketing and research, supervision of loans, data processing and
back office related activities.
Provided that for outsourced services relating to credit cards, the provisions set
out in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Credit Cards and
Debit Cards: Issuance and Conduct) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to
time, shall also apply.
(2) The provisions shall not apply to outsourcing of:

children.title: (i) IT services as defined in paragraph 48(1) of these Directions, unless specified
children.text:
(i) IT services as defined in paragraph 48(1) of these Directions, unless specified
otherwise in respective paragraphs of Chapter IV;

children.title: (ii) activities unrelated to banking services like usage of courier, catering of staff,
children.text:
(ii) activities unrelated to banking services like usage of courier, catering of staff,
housekeeping and janitorial services, security of the premises, and movement
and archiving of records.

children.title: 5. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter IV shall be as under:
children.text: 5. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter IV shall be as under:

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text:
C. Definitions
6.
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Outsourcing' means
use of a third party by a bank to perform activities on a continuing basis that would
normally be undertaken by the bank itself, now or in the future. 'Continuing basis'
shall include agreements for a limited period.
7.
Some other terms pertaining to outsourcing of financial services and IT services
have been defined in their respective Chapters as per their applicability.
8.
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank
of India Act, 1934 or the Information Technology Act, 2000 or the Companies Act,
2013 and Rules made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI or as used in commercial parlance,
as the case may be.

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Role of the Board
hierarchy.text:
Chapter II - Role of the Board
9.
The outsourcing of any service by a bank does not diminish its obligations, and those
of its Board and MD / CEO along with the Senior Management, who have the
ultimate responsibility for the outsourced service.

children.title: A. Board-Approved Policy
children.text: A. Board-Approved Policy

children.title: 10. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial or IT activities shall put in place
children.text:
10. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial or IT activities shall put in place
corresponding comprehensive Board-approved outsourcing policy, the coverage of
which is indicated in paragraph 20 and paragraph 52, respectively.

children.title: B. Key Responsibilities
children.text: B. Key Responsibilities

children.title: 11. The Board shall be responsible for putting in place a framework to evaluate the risks
children.text:
11. The Board shall be responsible for putting in place a framework to evaluate the risks
and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing arrangements, laying
down appropriate approval authorities depending on risks and materiality, and
undertaking regular review.

children.title: 12. In respect of outsourcing of financial services, the Board, or a Committee of the
children.text:
12. In respect of outsourcing of financial services, the Board, or a Committee of the
Board to which powers have been delegated, shall be responsible, inter alia, for:

children.title: (i) approving a framework to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending on
children.text:
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending on
risks and materiality;

children.title: (iii) undertaking regular review of the framework for its efficacy and updating the
(iv) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced, and
children.text:
(iv) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced, and
approving such arrangements;

children.title: (v) assessing management competencies to develop sound and responsive
children.text:
(v) assessing management competencies to develop sound and responsive
outsourcing risk management policies and procedures commensurate with the
nature, scope, and complexity of outsourcing arrangements; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) setting up suitable administrative framework of management;
children.text: (vi) setting up suitable administrative framework of management;

children.title: (vii) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half yearly basis;
children.text: (vii) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half yearly basis;

children.title: (viii) ensuring that a robust system of internal audit of all outsourced financial
children.text:
(viii) ensuring that a robust system of internal audit of all outsourced financial
activities is put in place and monitoring the same; and

children.title: (ix) ensuring submission of an Annual Compliance Certificate giving the particulars
children.text:
(ix) ensuring submission of an Annual Compliance Certificate giving the particulars
of contracts for outsourcing of financial services, the prescribed periodicity of
audit by internal or external auditor, major findings of the audit and action taken
to the Regional Offices of RBI.

children.title: 13. In respect of outsourcing of IT services, the Board shall be responsible, inter alia,
children.text:
13. In respect of outsourcing of IT services, the Board shall be responsible, inter alia,
for:

children.title: (i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing activities depending
children.text:
(i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing activities depending
on risks and materiality;

children.title: (ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
children.text:
(ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
prospective outsourcing arrangements;

children.title: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;
children.text: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;

children.title: (iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee that there is no conflict of
children.text:
(iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee that there is no conflict of

shall not be owned or controlled by any director, key managerial personnel,
approver of the outsourcing arrangement, or their relatives as indicated under

children.title: (v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior
children.text:
(v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services

children.title: A. Definitions
children.text: A. Definitions

children.title: 14. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Material Outsourcing'
children.text:
14. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Material Outsourcing'
means arrangements, which if disrupted, have the potential to significantly impact
the business operations, reputation or profitability of a bank. Materiality of
outsourcing shall be based on the:

children.title: (i) level of importance to the bank of the service being outsourced as well as the
children.text:
(i) level of importance to the bank of the service being outsourced as well as the
significance of the risk posed by the same;

children.title: (ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the bank on various parameters such as
children.text:
(ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the bank on various parameters such as
earnings, solvency, liquidity, funding capital and risk profile;

children.title: (iii) likely impact on the bank's reputation and brand value, and ability to achieve
(iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the bank;
children.text: (iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the bank;

children.title: (v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the bank
children.text:
(v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the bank
outsources various functions to the same service provider; and

children.title: (vi) significance of activities outsourced by the bank in context of customer service
children.text:
(vi) significance of activities outsourced by the bank in context of customer service
and protection.

children.title: B. Activities that shall not be outsourced
children.text: B. Activities that shall not be outsourced

children.title: 15. A bank which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not outsource
children.text:
15. A bank which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not outsource
core management functions including policy formulation, Internal Audit and
compliance, compliance with KYC norms, credit sanction and management of
investment portfolio.
Provided that where required, experts, including former employees, could be hired
on a contractual basis subject to:

children.title: (i) the Audit Committee of Board (ACB) / Board being assured that such expertise
children.text:
(i) the Audit Committee of Board (ACB) / Board being assured that such expertise
does not exist within the audit function of the bank;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) any conflict of interest in such matters being recognised and effectively
children.text:
(ii) any conflict of interest in such matters being recognised and effectively
addressed; and

children.title: (iii) ownership of audit reports in all cases resting with regular functionaries of the
children.text:
(iii) ownership of audit reports in all cases resting with regular functionaries of the
internal audit function.

children.title: C. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text: C. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight

children.title: 16. A bank, which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior approval
children.text:
16. A bank, which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior approval
from RBI. However, such arrangements shall be subject to on-site / off-site
monitoring and inspection / scrutiny by RBI.

children.title: 17. As stated in paragraph 9, the outsourcing of any activity by a bank shall not diminish
children.text:
17. As stated in paragraph 9, the outsourcing of any activity by a bank shall not diminish
its obligations including to its customers and RBI, and those of its Board and MD /
CEO along with the Senior Management, who have the ultimate responsibility for
the outsourced activity. The bank shall, therefore, be responsible for the actions of
its service provider including Business Correspondents and their retail outlets / sub-
agents and the confidentiality of information pertaining to the customers that is
available with the service provider. The bank shall retain ultimate control of the
outsourced activity.

children.title: 18. A bank shall ensure that:
children.text: 18. A bank shall ensure that:

children.title: (i) all relevant laws, regulations, rules, guidelines and conditions of approval,
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
(iii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of a bank's
children.text:
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of a bank's
internal control, business conduct, or reputation;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing the
children.text:
(v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing the
services as would be employed by the bank, if the activities were conducted
within the bank and not outsourced; and

children.title: (vi) the service provider shall not be owned or controlled by any director or officer
children.text:
(vi) the service provider shall not be owned or controlled by any director or officer
/ employee of the bank or their relatives having the same meaning as assigned
under Companies Act, 2013 and the Rules framed thereunder, as amended
from time to time.

children.title: 19. A bank shall be responsible for making Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs) and
children.text:
19. A bank shall be responsible for making Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs) and
Suspicious Transactions Reports (STRs) to FIU or any other competent authority in
respect of its customer related activities carried out by the service providers.

children.title: D. Governance Framework
children.text:
D. Governance Framework
D.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 20. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
children.text:
20. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
comprehensive outsourcing policy, approved by its Board, which shall incorporate,
inter alia, the following:

children.title: (i) criteria for selection of such activities, and service providers;
children.text: (i) criteria for selection of such activities, and service providers;

children.title: (ii) parameters for defining 'material outsourcing' based on the broad criteria
children.text:
(ii) parameters for defining 'material outsourcing' based on the broad criteria
indicated in paragraph 14 of these Directions;

children.title: (iii) delegation of authority depending on risks and materiality; and
children.text: (iii) delegation of authority depending on risks and materiality; and

children.title: (iv) systems to monitor and review these activities.
children.text:
(iv) systems to monitor and review these activities.
D.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 21. The Managing Director (MD) / Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Senior
children.text:
21. The Managing Director (MD) / Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Senior
Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible for:

children.title: (i) evaluating the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing,
children.text:
(i) evaluating the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing,
based on the framework approved by the Board;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) developing and implementing sound and prudent outsourcing policies and
(iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;
children.text: (iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;

children.title: (iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the
children.text:
(iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the
Board in a timely manner;

children.title: (v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
children.text:
(v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
scenarios, are in place and tested;

children.title: (vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
children.text:
(vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
policies; and

children.title: (vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
children.text:
(vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
material outsourcing risks.

children.title: E. Risk Management
children.text:
E. Risk Management
E.1 Evaluation of the Risks

children.title: 22. A bank shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering into
children.text:
22. A bank shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering into
outsourcing arrangements:

children.title: (i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on its
children.text:
(i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on its
own behalf, inconsistent with the overall strategic goals of the bank.

children.title: (ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service,
children.text:
(ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service,
or its customer interactions are inconsistent with the overall standards of the
bank, or it fails to preserve and protect confidential customer information.

children.title: (iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
children.text:
(iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
consumer, and prudential laws are not adequately complied with.

children.title: (iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error, or
children.text:
(iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error, or
inadequate financial capacity of the service provider to fulfil obligations or to
provide remedies.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) Legal Risk - where a bank is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
children.text:
(v) Legal Risk - where a bank is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
punitive damages resulting from supervisory actions, or private settlements
due to omissions and commissions by the service provider.

children.title: (vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when a bank becomes over reliant on one
children.text:
(vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when a bank becomes over reliant on one
service provider, loses relevant internal skills preventing it from bringing the
activity back in-house, or enters into contracts that make speedy exits
prohibitively expensive.

children.title: (vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
children.text:
(vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
inappropriate underwriting or credit assessments.

children.title: (viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk
children.text:
(viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk
in the outsourcing arrangement.

children.title: (ix) Contractual Risk - where the bank may not have the ability to enforce the
children.text:
(ix) Contractual Risk - where the bank may not have the ability to enforce the
contract with the service provider.

children.title: (x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of a bank
children.text:
(x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of a bank
over a service provider, more so when overall banking industry has
considerable exposure to one service provider.
E.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 23. A bank shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
children.text:
23. A bank shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
customer information in the custody of the service provider.

children.title: 24. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a 'need
children.text:
24. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a 'need
to know' basis, i.e., limited to those areas where the information is required in order
to perform the outsourced function.

children.title: 25. A bank shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
children.text:
25. A bank shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
service providers on a regular basis and require the service provider to disclose
security breaches.

children.title: 26. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
children.text:
26. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
entities, the bank shall take care to build strong safeguards so that there is no
comingling or combining of information, documents, records, and assets.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 27. A bank shall ensure that a service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify the
children.text:
27. A bank shall ensure that a service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify the
bank's customer information, documents, records and assets to protect the
confidentiality of the information.

children.title: 28. A bank shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage
children.text:
28. A bank shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage
of confidential customer related information. In these eventualities, the bank shall
be liable to its customers for any damage.

children.title: F. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
F. Outsourcing Process
F.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 29. A bank shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
children.text:
29. A bank shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
arrangement are carefully defined in written agreements and vetted by the bank's
legal counsel on their legal effect and enforceability. The agreement shall
appropriately reckon the associated risks and the strategies for mitigating or
managing them. The bank shall ensure that such an agreement is sufficiently flexible
to allow it to retain an appropriate level of control over the outsourcing and the right
to intervene with appropriate measures to meet legal and regulatory obligations. The
agreement shall also bring out the nature of legal relationship between the parties,
i.e., whether agent-principal or otherwise.

children.title: 30. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:
children.text: 30. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:

children.title: (i) details of the service being outsourced including Service Level Agreements
children.text:
(i) details of the service being outsourced including Service Level Agreements
(SLAs) to agree and establish accountability for performance expectations.
SLAs shall clearly formalise the performance criteria to measure the quality
and quantity of service levels;

children.title: (ii) bank's access to all books, records, and information relevant to the outsourced
children.text:
(ii) bank's access to all books, records, and information relevant to the outsourced
service available with the service provider;

children.title: (iii) regular and continuous monitoring and assessment by the bank of the service
(iv) prior approval or consent by the bank for the use of subcontractors by the
children.text:
(iv) prior approval or consent by the bank for the use of subcontractors by the
service provider for all or part of an outsourced service. Before according the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
consent, a bank shall review the subcontracting arrangement and ensure that
these arrangements are compliant with these Directions;

children.title: (v) controls for maintaining confidentiality of data including of its customers, and
(vi) contingency plans to ensure business continuity;
children.text: (vi) contingency plans to ensure business continuity;

children.title: (vii) bank's right to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal or
children.text:
(vii) bank's right to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal or
external auditors, or by agents appointed to act on its behalf and to obtain
copies of any audit or review reports and findings made on the service provider
in conjunction with the services performed for the bank;

children.title: (viii) right of RBI or persons authorised by it to access the bank's documents,
children.text:
(viii) right of RBI or persons authorised by it to access the bank's documents,
records of transactions, logs, and other necessary information given to, stored
or processed by the service provider within a reasonable time. This includes
information maintained in paper and electronic formats;

children.title: (ix) right of the RBI to cause an inspection of a service provider of a bank and its
(x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination;
children.text: (x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination;

children.title: (xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained
children.text:
(xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained
even after the contract expires or gets terminated; and

children.title: (xii) provisions to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data
children.text:
(xii) provisions to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data
in accordance with legal and regulatory obligations of the bank and take
suitable steps to ensure that its interests are protected in this regard even post
termination of the services.
F.3 Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Activities

children.title: 31. A bank shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
children.text:
31. A bank shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourced activities and shall ensure that outsourcing agreements with its service
providers contain provisions to address the same.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 32. A bank shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial services
children.text:
32. A bank shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial services
for review by its Board and MD / CEO along with the Senior Management. The
records shall be updated promptly, and half-yearly reviews shall be placed before
the Board.

children.title: 33. Regular audits, at least annually, by either the internal auditors or external auditors
children.text:
33. Regular audits, at least annually, by either the internal auditors or external auditors
of a bank shall assess the adequacy of the risk management practices adopted in
overseeing and managing the outsourcing arrangement, the bank's compliance with
its risk management framework, and the requirements of these Directions.

children.title: 34. A bank shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
children.text:
34. A bank shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
condition of the service provider to assess its ability to continue to meet its
outsourcing obligations. Such due diligence reviews, which shall be based on all
available information about the service provider, shall highlight any deterioration or
breach in performance standards, confidentiality, and security, and in operational
resilience or business continuity preparedness.

children.title: 35. Certain services might involve reconciliation of transaction between a bank and the
children.text:
35. Certain services might involve reconciliation of transaction between a bank and the
service providers (or its subcontractors). In such cases, the bank shall ensure that
reconciliation of transactions between itself and the service provider (or its
subcontractors) is carried out as advised in RBI guidelines on 'Outsourcing of Cash
Management - Reconciliation of Transactions' dated May 14, 2019, as applicable,
and amended from time to time.
F.4 Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan

children.title: 36. A bank shall require its service provider to develop and establish a robust framework
children.text:
36. A bank shall require its service provider to develop and establish a robust framework
for documenting, maintaining and testing business continuity and recovery
procedures. The bank shall ensure that the service provider periodically tests the
Business Continuity and Recovery Plan, and may also consider joint testing and
recovery exercises with its service provider at mutually agreed frequency but at least
annually.

children.title: 37. In establishing a viable contingency plan, a bank shall consider the availability of
children.text:
37. In establishing a viable contingency plan, a bank shall consider the availability of
alternative service providers or the possibility of bringing the outsourced activity

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
back in-house in an emergency and the costs, time and resources that would be
involved.

children.title: 38. A bank shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the bank's
children.text:
38. A bank shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the bank's
information, documents, and records, and other assets so that in adverse conditions
or termination of the agreement, all documents, records of transactions and
information given to the service provider and assets of the bank can be removed
from the possession of the service provider (in order to enable the bank to continue
its business operations); or deleted, destroyed or rendered unusable.

children.title: 39. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing agreement
children.text:
39. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing agreement
or insolvency or liquidation of its service provider, a bank shall retain an appropriate
level of control over its outsourcing arrangement along with the right to intervene
with appropriate measures to continue its business operations without incurring
prohibitive expenses and disruption in the operations of the bank and its services to
the customers.
F.5 Termination

children.title: 40. If the services of a service provider are terminated by a bank, then it shall:
children.text: 40. If the services of a service provider are terminated by a bank, then it shall:

children.title: (i) publicise the same by displaying at a prominent place in the branches and
children.text:
(i) publicise the same by displaying at a prominent place in the branches and
posting it on the website so as to ensure that its customers do not continue to
deal with the service provider; and

children.title: (ii) inform Indian Banks' Association (IBA) of the reasons for termination to enable
children.text:
(ii) inform Indian Banks' Association (IBA) of the reasons for termination to enable
IBA to maintain a caution list of such service providers for sharing among
banks.

children.title: G. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
children.text:
G. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
G.1 Offshore outsourcing

children.title: 41. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
children.text:
41. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
operating in jurisdictions that uphold confidentiality agreements and clauses.

children.title: 42. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, a bank shall:
children.text: 42. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, a bank shall:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
children.text:
(i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
provider is based and political, social, economic, and legal conditions, both
during the risk assessment and on a continuous basis, and establish sound
procedures for dealing with country risk. This includes having appropriate
contingency and exit strategies;

children.title: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;
children.text: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (iii) ensure that the availability of records to the bank and RBI will not be affected
children.text:
(iii) ensure that the availability of records to the bank and RBI will not be affected
even in the case of liquidation of the service provider or offshore custodian;

children.title: (iv) ensure activities outsourced outside India are conducted in a manner so as not
(v) ensure that, where the offshore service provider is a regulated entity, the
(vi) ensure that the regulatory authority of the offshore location does not have
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
children.text:
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
maintained does not extend to the operations of the bank in India on the
strength of the fact that the data is being processed there even though the
actual transactions are undertaken in India; and

children.title: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.
children.text: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.

children.title: 43. The overseas operations of a bank shall be governed by both, these Directions and
children.text:
43. The overseas operations of a bank shall be governed by both, these Directions and
the host country guidelines, and in case there are differences, the more stringent of
the two shall prevail. However, where there is any conflict, the host country
guidelines shall prevail.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: H. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services
children.text: H. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services

children.title: 44. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank shall not affect the rights of its
children.text:
44. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank shall not affect the rights of its
customers against the bank, including the ability of the customers to obtain redressal
as applicable under relevant laws.

children.title: 45. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the process
children.text:
45. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the process
of dealing with a bank, the bank shall incorporate a clause in the corresponding
product literature, and brochures, stating that services of the service provider,
including in sales, and marketing, of the products may be used. The role of the
service provider may be indicated in broad terms.

children.title: 46. A bank shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
children.text:
46. A bank shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
compromised in any manner on account of outsourcing, i.e., responsibility for
redressal of customers' grievances related to outsourced services shall rest with the
bank. In case of microfinance loans, a declaration that, (i) the bank shall be
accountable for inappropriate behaviour by its employees or employees of the
outsourced agency and (ii) shall provide timely grievance redressal, shall be made
in the loan agreement, and also in the Fair Practices Code (FPC) displayed in its
office, branch premises, and its website.

children.title: 47. In addition to the above,
children.text:
47. In addition to the above,
(1) a bank shall constitute Grievance Redressal Machinery in accordance with the
provisions of Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Responsible
Business Conduct) Directions, 2025, and give wide publicity about it within the
bank through electronic and print media and also by placing the information on
its website;
(2) the name and contact number of designated grievance redressal officer of the
bank shall be made known and widely publicised. The designated officer shall
ensure that genuine grievances of customers are redressed promptly without
involving delay. It shall be clearly indicated that bank's Grievance Redressal
Machinery will also deal with the issue relating to services provided by the service
provider;

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(3) the grievance redressal procedure of the bank and the time frame for responding
to the complaints (maximum 30 days) shall be placed on the bank's website; and
(4) if a complaint was rejected wholly or partly by a bank and the complainant is not
satisfied with the reply or does not get any reply within 30 days, after the bank
received the complaint, the complainant shall have the following options for
redressal of their grievance(s):

children.title: (i) the RBI's Ombudsman in case of banks to which RBI's Integrated
children.text:
(i) the RBI's Ombudsman in case of banks to which RBI's Integrated
Ombudsman Scheme, 2021 applies, or

children.title: (ii) Consumer Education and Protection Cell (CEPC) of respective Regional
children.text:
(ii) Consumer Education and Protection Cell (CEPC) of respective Regional
Office of RBI in case of banks to which RBI's Integrated Ombudsman
Scheme, 2021 does not apply.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter IV - Outsourcing of Information Technology (IT) Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter IV - Outsourcing of Information Technology (IT) Services

children.title: A. Definitions
children.text: A. Definitions

children.title: 48. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall bear
children.text:
48. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall bear
the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'IT services' means IT services / IT enabled services / IT activities.
(2) 'Material Outsourcing of IT Services' are those which:

children.title: (i) if disrupted or compromised shall have the potential to significantly
children.text:
(i) if disrupted or compromised shall have the potential to significantly
impact the bank's business operations; or

children.title: (ii) may have material impact on the bank's customers in the event of any
children.text:
(ii) may have material impact on the bank's customers in the event of any
unauthorised access, loss or theft of customer information;
(3) 'Service Provider' means provider of IT services including entities related
to the bank.
Provided that, for the purpose of this Chapter, the following indicative (but
not exhaustive) list of vendors and entities shall not be considered as
'Service Providers' as defined above:

children.title: (i) vendors providing business services using IT (e.g., BCs);
children.text: (i) vendors providing business services using IT (e.g., BCs);

children.title: (ii) Payment System Operators (PSOs) authorised by RBI under the
(iii) partnership based FinTech firms such as those providing co-branded
(iv) FinTech firms providing services for data retrieval, and data
children.text:
(iv) FinTech firms providing services for data retrieval, and data
validation and verification such as, bank statement analysis, GST
returns analysis, fetching of vehicle information, digital document
execution, data entry, and call centre services;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) telecom service providers from whom leased lines or other similar
(vi) security or audit consultants appointed for certification, audit or VA /
children.text:
(vi) security or audit consultants appointed for certification, audit or VA /
PT related to IT infrastructure, IT services, or Information Security
services, in their role as independent third-party auditor, consultant,
or lead implementer.

children.title: Explanation: In case an IT service is provided by an RE to a bank (as
children.text:
Explanation: In case an IT service is provided by an RE to a bank (as
applicable to the scope of Directions under this Chapter), even the RE could
be considered as a service provider of the bank, under this Chapter.
(4) 'Sub-contractor' refers to those providing material / significant IT services
to the service provider and is specific to the material / significant IT services
arrangement that the bank has entered into with the service provider.

children.title: B. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text: B. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight

children.title: 49. In addition to adhering to the requirements stipulated in subparagraphs (i) to (v) of
children.text:
49. In addition to adhering to the requirements stipulated in subparagraphs (i) to (v) of
paragraph 18 of these Directions, a bank shall ensure that the service provider shall
not be owned or controlled by any director, or key managerial personnel, or approver
of the outsourcing arrangement of the bank, or their relatives. The terms 'control',
'director', 'key managerial personnel', and 'relative' shall have the same meaning as
assigned under the Companies Act, 2013 and the Rules framed thereunder from
time to time.
Provided that, an exception to the above requirement may be made with the
approval of Board or a Committee of the Board, followed by appropriate disclosure,
oversight and monitoring of such arrangements.

children.title: 50. A bank shall evaluate the need for outsourcing of IT services based on a
children.text:
50. A bank shall evaluate the need for outsourcing of IT services based on a
comprehensive assessment of attendant benefits, risks and availability of
commensurate processes to manage those risks. For this purpose, the bank shall,
inter alia, consider the following:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) the need for outsourcing based on materiality / criticality of service to be
children.text:
(i) the need for outsourcing based on materiality / criticality of service to be
outsourced;

children.title: (ii) expectations and outcomes from outsourcing;
children.text: (ii) expectations and outcomes from outsourcing;

children.title: (iii) success factors and cost-benefit analysis; and
children.text: (iii) success factors and cost-benefit analysis; and

children.title: (iv) the model for outsourcing.
children.text: (iv) the model for outsourcing.

children.title: 51. A bank shall ensure that cyber incidents are reported to it by the service provider
children.text:
51. A bank shall ensure that cyber incidents are reported to it by the service provider
without undue delay, so that an incident is reported by the bank to the RBI within six
hours of detection by the service provider.

children.title: C. Governance Framework
children.text:
C. Governance Framework
C.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 52. A bank intending to outsource any of its IT services shall put in place a
children.text:
52. A bank intending to outsource any of its IT services shall put in place a
comprehensive Board approved IT outsourcing policy incorporating, inter alia, the
following:

children.title: (i) the roles and responsibilities of the Board, Committees of the Board (if any)
(ii) criteria for selection of such services and service providers;
children.text: (ii) criteria for selection of such services and service providers;

children.title: (iii) parameters for defining material outsourcing based on the broad criteria
children.text:
(iii) parameters for defining material outsourcing based on the broad criteria
defined in paragraph 48(2) of these Directions;

children.title: (iv) delegation of authority depending on risk and materiality;
children.text: (iv) delegation of authority depending on risk and materiality;

children.title: (v) disaster recovery and business continuity plans;
children.text: (v) disaster recovery and business continuity plans;

children.title: (vi) systems to monitor and review the operations of these services ; and
children.text: (vi) systems to monitor and review the operations of these services ; and

children.title: (vii) termination processes and exit strategies, including business continuity in the
children.text:
(vii) termination processes and exit strategies, including business continuity in the
event of a service provider exiting the outsourcing arrangement.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
F.1 Service Provider Evaluation

children.title: 53. A bank shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
children.text:
53. A bank shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
outsourcing arrangement, to assess the capability of the service provider to comply
with obligations in the outsourcing agreement on an ongoing basis.

children.title: 54. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
children.text:
54. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
information, as applicable, about the service provider, including but not limited to:

children.title: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;
children.text: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;

children.title: (ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
children.text:
(ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
provider or a limited number of service providers;

children.title: (iii) past experience and demonstrated competence to implement and support the
children.text:
(iii) past experience and demonstrated competence to implement and support the
proposed activity over the contracted period;

children.title: (iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
children.text:
(iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
conditions;

children.title: (v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints and outstanding or
children.text:
(v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints and outstanding or
potential litigation;

children.title: (vi) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees and
children.text:
(vi) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees and
sub-contractors;

children.title: (vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting and monitoring
children.text:
(vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting and monitoring
procedures, and business continuity management;

children.title: (viii) external factors like political, economic, social, and legal environment of the
children.text:
(viii) external factors like political, economic, social, and legal environment of the
jurisdiction in which the service provider operates and other events that may
impact data security and service performance; and

children.title: (ix) ability to effectively service all the customers with confidentiality, especially
children.text:
(ix) ability to effectively service all the customers with confidentiality, especially
where a service provider has exposure to multiple entities.

children.title: 55. Where possible, a bank shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
children.text:
55. Where possible, a bank shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
the service provider to supplement the findings of its own due diligence.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 56. A bank shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
children.text:
56. A bank shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
compatible with those of the bank, and acceptability of their standards of
performance including in the area of customer service.
C.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 57. The MD / CEO and Senior Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible
children.text:
57. The MD / CEO and Senior Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible
for:

children.title: (i) formulating IT outsourcing policies and procedures, evaluating the risks and
children.text:
(i) formulating IT outsourcing policies and procedures, evaluating the risks and
materiality of all existing and prospective IT outsourcing arrangements based
on the framework commensurate with the complexity, nature and scope, in line
with the enterprise-wide risk management of the bank approved by the Board
and its implementation;

children.title: (ii) prior evaluation of prospective IT outsourcing arrangements and periodic
children.text:
(ii) prior evaluation of prospective IT outsourcing arrangements and periodic
evaluation of the existing outsourcing arrangements covering the performance
review, criticality and associated risks of all such arrangements based on the
policy approved by the Board;

children.title: (iii) identifying IT outsourcing risks as they arise, monitoring, mitigating, managing
(iv) ensuring that suitable business continuity plans based on realistic and
(v) ensuring (a) effective oversight over the service provider (and its
(vi) ensuring an independent review and audit on a periodic basis for compliance
children.text:
(vi) ensuring an independent review and audit on a periodic basis for compliance
with the legislations, regulations, Board-approved policy and performance
standards and reporting the same to Board or a Committee of the Board; and

children.title: (vii) creating essential capacity with required skillsets within the bank for proper
children.text:
(vii) creating essential capacity with required skillsets within the bank for proper
oversight of outsourced services.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
C.3 Role of IT Function

children.title: 58. The responsibilities of the IT Function of a bank shall, inter alia, include:
children.text:
58. The responsibilities of the IT Function of a bank shall, inter alia, include:
(1) assisting the Senior Management in identifying, measuring, monitoring,
mitigating and managing the level of IT outsourcing risk in the bank;
(2) ensuring that a central database of all IT outsourcing arrangements is
maintained and is accessible for review by Board, Senior Management,
auditors, and supervisors;
(3) effectively monitoring and supervising the outsourced IT service to ensure that
the service provider meets the laid down performance standards and provides
uninterrupted services, reporting to the Senior Management, co-ordinating
periodic due diligence, and highlighting concerns, if any; and
(4) putting in place necessary documentation required for contractual agreements
including service level management, monitoring of vendor operations, key risk
indicators and classifying the vendors as per the determined risk.

children.title: D. Risk Management
children.text:
D. Risk Management
D.1 Risk Management Framework

children.title: 59. A bank shall put in place a risk management framework that comprehensively deals
children.text:
59. A bank shall put in place a risk management framework that comprehensively deals
with the processes and responsibilities for identification, measurement, mitigation,
management, and reporting of risks associated with such IT outsourcing
arrangements.

children.title: 60. A bank shall suitably document risk assessments with necessary approvals in line
children.text:
60. A bank shall suitably document risk assessments with necessary approvals in line
with the roles and responsibilities of the Board of Directors, Senior Management and
IT Function and subject the same to internal and external quality assurance on a
periodic basis as determined by the Board-approved policy.

children.title: 61. A bank shall effectively assess the impact of concentration risk posed by multiple
children.text:
61. A bank shall effectively assess the impact of concentration risk posed by multiple
outsourcing arrangements to the same service provider and the concentration risk
posed by outsourcing critical or material functions to a limited number of service
providers.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
D.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 62. A bank shall be responsible for the confidentiality and integrity of data and
children.text:
62. A bank shall be responsible for the confidentiality and integrity of data and
information pertaining to its customers that are available to the service provider.

children.title: 63. In this regard, a bank shall adhere to directions stated in paragraph 23 to paragraph
children.text:
63. In this regard, a bank shall adhere to directions stated in paragraph 23 to paragraph
26 of these Directions and additionally ensure that:

children.title: (i) access by service providers to data at the bank or its data centre shall be on
(ii) in the event of multiple service provider relationships where two or more
children.text:
(ii) in the event of multiple service provider relationships where two or more
service providers collaborate to deliver an end-to-end solution, the bank
remains responsible for understanding and monitoring the control environment
of all service providers that have access to its data, systems, records or
resources;

children.title: (iii) it immediately notifies RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage of
children.text:
(iii) it immediately notifies RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage of
confidential customer related information. In these eventualities, the bank shall
adhere to the extant instructions issued by RBI from time to time on Incident
Response and Recovery Management.

children.title: 64. With regard to requirement that the data should not be combined or comingled, as
children.text:
64. With regard to requirement that the data should not be combined or comingled, as
stipulated in paragraph 26, it would suffice if there is clear separation and isolation
of data (bank and its customer specific data and information) to ensure that only the
personnel as authorised by the bank is able to access data that belongs to them in
a multi-tenant environment / architecture.

children.title: E. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
E. Outsourcing Process
E.1 Service Provider Evaluation

children.title: 65. The directions regarding service provider evaluation as applicable to outsourcing of
children.text:
65. The directions regarding service provider evaluation as applicable to outsourcing of
financial services contained in paragraph 53 to paragraph 55 shall apply, mutatis
mutandis, to outsourcing of IT services, with the following additional considerations:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) technology, infrastructural stability, data backup arrangements, and disaster
children.text:
(i) technology, infrastructural stability, data backup arrangements, and disaster
recovery plan;

children.title: (ii) conflict of interest, if any;
children.text: (ii) conflict of interest, if any;

children.title: (iii) capability to identify, and segregate bank's data;
children.text: (iii) capability to identify, and segregate bank's data;

children.title: (iv) capability to comply with the regulatory and legal requirements of the
children.text:
(iv) capability to comply with the regulatory and legal requirements of the
outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (v) information / cyber security risk assessment;
children.text: (v) information / cyber security risk assessment;

children.title: (vi) ensuring that appropriate controls, assurance requirements, and possible
children.text:
(vi) ensuring that appropriate controls, assurance requirements, and possible
contractual arrangements are in place to ensure data protection and bank's
access to the data which is processed, managed or stored by the service
provider;

children.title: (vii) ability to effectively service all the customers while maintaining confidentiality,
children.text:
(vii) ability to effectively service all the customers while maintaining confidentiality,
especially where a service provider has exposure to multiple entities; and

children.title: (viii) ability to enforce agreements and the rights available thereunder including
children.text:
(viii) ability to enforce agreements and the rights available thereunder including
those relating to aspects such as data storage, data protection, and
confidentiality.

children.title: 66. A bank should adopt risk-based approach in conducting such due diligence
children.text:
66. A bank should adopt risk-based approach in conducting such due diligence
activities.
E.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 67. A bank shall ensure that its rights and obligations and those of each service provider
children.text:
67. A bank shall ensure that its rights and obligations and those of each service provider
are clearly defined and set out in a legally binding written agreement, in line with the
provisions specified in paragraph 29 of these Directions. In principle, the provisions
of the agreement shall appropriately reckon the criticality of the outsourced task to
the business of the bank, the associated risks and the strategies for mitigating or
managing them.

children.title: 68. In addition to the requirements specified in subparagraphs (i) to (vii) of paragraph
children.text:
68. In addition to the requirements specified in subparagraphs (i) to (vii) of paragraph
30, a bank shall also include at minimum (as applicable to the scope of Directions

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
in this Chapter) the following aspects in any agreement for outsourcing of IT
services:

children.title: (i) provisions covering service provider's subcontractors with respect to service
(ii) access by the bank to all data, books, records, information logs, alerts and
(iii) type of material adverse events (e.g., data breaches, denial of service, and
children.text:
(iii) type of material adverse events (e.g., data breaches, denial of service, and
service unavailability, relevant to the outsourced services) and the incidents
required to be reported to the bank to enable the bank to take prompt risk
mitigation measures and ensure compliance with statutory and regulatory
guidelines;

children.title: (iv) compliance with the provisions of Information Technology Act, 2000, and other
children.text:
(iv) compliance with the provisions of Information Technology Act, 2000, and other
applicable legal requirements and standards to protect the customer data;

children.title: (v) the deliverables including SLAs formalising performance criteria to measure
children.text:
(v) the deliverables including SLAs formalising performance criteria to measure
the quality and quantity of service levels;

children.title: (vi) storage of data only in India (as applicable) as per extant regulatory
children.text:
(vi) storage of data only in India (as applicable) as per extant regulatory
requirements;

children.title: (vii) clauses requiring the service provider to provide details of data (related to the
children.text:
(vii) clauses requiring the service provider to provide details of data (related to the
bank and its customers) captured, processed, and stored;

children.title: (viii) types of data / information that the service provider (vendor) is permitted to
children.text:
(viii) types of data / information that the service provider (vendor) is permitted to
share with bank's customer and / or any other party;

children.title: (ix) the resolution process, events of default, indemnities, remedies, and recourse
children.text:
(ix) the resolution process, events of default, indemnities, remedies, and recourse
available to the respective parties;

children.title: (x) contingency plan(s) to ensure testing requirements;
children.text: (x) contingency plan(s) to ensure testing requirements;

children.title: (xi) right to seek information from the service provider about the third parties (in
children.text:
(xi) right to seek information from the service provider about the third parties (in
the supply chain) engaged by the former;

children.title: <image>

children.title: (xii) right of RBI or person(s) authorised by it to perform inspection of the service
children.text:
(xii) right of RBI or person(s) authorised by it to perform inspection of the service
provider and any of its sub-contractors and access the bank's IT infrastructure,
applications, data, documents, and other necessary information given to,
stored or processed by the service provider / or its sub-contractors in relation
and as applicable to the scope of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (xiii) clauses making the service provider contractually liable for the performance
children.text:
(xiii) clauses making the service provider contractually liable for the performance
and risk management practices of its subcontractors;

children.title: (xiv) obligation of the service provider to comply with directions issued by the RBI
(xv) termination rights of the bank, including the ability to orderly transfer the
(xvi) obligation of the service provider to co-operate with the relevant authorities in
children.text:
(xvi) obligation of the service provider to co-operate with the relevant authorities in
